title: Iran-US War Negotiations Divert US Focus from Gaza Phase Two
detail: As of March 27, 2026, the Trump administration's primary diplomatic focus has shifted to Iran-Israel war negotiations. The White House confirmed ongoing talks with Iran regarding a 15-point plan to end the Iran-Israel conflict, with discussions about arranging a meeting in Pakistan. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi emphasized that message exchanges with the US do not constitute formal negotiations. This strategic pivot has effectively sidelined Gaza Phase Two implementation. US envoy Gal Hirsch traveled to Cairo for talks on advancing Phase Two of the Gaza ceasefire and hostage deal framework, with limited results. The Gaza Phase Two impasse deepens as Hamas maintains its grip on civilian administration and Israel refuses withdrawal before demilitarization.

title: All Hostages Accounted For — Crisis Fully Resolved as of January 2026
detail: As of March 27, 2026, no Israeli hostages remain in Gaza. The 902-day hostage crisis came to its conclusion on January 26, 2026, when IDF forces recovered the remains of Master Sgt. Ran Gvili — a slain police officer and the last of the 251 hostages taken on October 7, 2023 — from a cemetery in northern Gaza. Final accounting: 168 returned alive, 85 bodies repatriated. Alive returns included: 8 rescued by IDF military operations, 5 released outside ceasefire frameworks, 105 returned in the November 2023 temporary truce, 30 in the January 2025 ceasefire Phase One, and 20 final living hostages released October 13, 2025 under Trump's 20-point peace plan. This marked the first time since 2014 that Hamas holds no Israeli captives.
